Fix was additive: 4 files patched (compose dev + compose deploy + bundle staging in scripts/commands/config.sh + bundle test sandbox symlink in internal/deploy/bundle_secret_contract_test.go). SCOPE-04 13/13 core DoD + Build Quality grouped now ticked. Spec 061 stays in_progress — 6 scopes remain (SCOPE-05 In Progress; SCOPE-06/07/08/09/10 Not Started; SCOPE-06/07/08 blocked by cross-spec packets to specs 054 + 060 which are OUT-OF-BOUNDS per user instruction). Workflow emits route_required for SCOPE-05 follow-up + cross-spec packets to specs 054 + 060.

executionHistory.summary: Round 74 (bubbles.plan; SCOPE-06c plan-triage of Round 73 finding SCOPE-06C-CPU-INTERACTIVE-15S-INFEASIBLE). Claim Source: advisory (planning decision; one read-only verification round against current SST surface). Outcome: route_required. Decision: option (c) operator-defer cpu-tier live-stack DoD #5 to accel-tier host. Rejected (a) cap num_predict + narrow top-K + drop retries (verified against current code: no per-tier num_predict SST key exists; threading it through agent runtime is structural, not knob-only as orchestrator framing assumed; schema_retry_budget already 1; assistant.skills.retrieval.top_k is global not per-tier; cheap subset (top_k 8->3) saves ~1-3s leaving razor-thin 12-14s budget on a contract that should be deterministic). Rejected (b) smaller cpu-tier model (coin-flip on citation-following preservation; no measurement evidence; loop-framework overhead ~3-5s not addressed by model swap alone). Rejected hybrid (a)->(c) (sunk cost; option (a) plumbing has no other consumer). Rejected anti-option (d) mock-LLM (loses entire reason BS-002/BS-007 exist). Selected (c) parallels DoD #6's already-accepted operator-defer pattern, matches cpu-tier's scoped 'dev-loop ergonomic, not production-quality' purpose, recommended by implement subagent who ran the RED 4 times. Measurable success criteria for closing DoD #5 via (c): PACKET 3 to bubbles.design lands design.md §5.1 amendment recording the operator-defer outcome; PACKET 4 to bubbles.implement lands test-runner skip mechanism in tests/e2e/assistant_bs00{2,7}_test.sh keyed on SMACKEREL_HARDWARE_TIER=cpu + docs/Testing.md tier-gated-live-stack-tests subsection. Once #1+#2 land, DoD #5 flips to [x] with operator-defer annotation, DoD #7 flips after SCOPE-06 unblock note written against accel-tier preconditions, SCOPE-06c promotes to Done with two operator-deferred items (#5 + #6). Owned edits this round: (1) specs/061-conversational-assistant/scopes.md — SCOPE-06c Status line updated to 'Blocked-pending-design-amendment (Round 74 plan-triage — option (c) selected)'; DoD #5 annotation rewritten to record option-(c) decision + measurable-success-criteria pointer; (2) specs/061-conversational-assistant/report.md — Round 74 section appended with anchor #round-74-scope-06c-plan-triage capturing D7 evidence verbatim, SST-surface audit, decision matrix, PACKETs 3+4, SKIP_PII_SCAN audit (verdict TRUE / no action), [F061-HARSDWARE-TIER-MISSING] typo audit (verdict FALSE / no fix); (3) specs/061-conversational-assistant/state.json — this Round 74 entry prepended (also folds Round 73 entry from working tree into committed history); execution.lastUpdatedAt bump. executionHistory.summary: Round 73 (bubbles.implement; SCOPE-06c DoD #5 cpu-tier live-stack RE-RUN — RED). Claim Source: executed. Outcome: route_required. Trigger: Round 71d deferred DoD #5 to bubbles.test; executed inline this round against HEAD c1ee3614 + Round 71d working tree. Actions: (1) verified `.smackerel.local.env` SMACKEREL_HARDWARE_TIER=cpu; (2) `./smackerel.sh --env test config generate` re-resolved test.env with cpu cell (qwen2.5:0.5b-instruct@15000ms); (3) `./smackerel.sh --env test build` --no-cache rebuilt smackerel-test-smackerel-core image to pick up internal/agent/loader.go ExpandEnv + scripts/runtime/scenario-lint.sh env propagation; (4) `./smackerel.sh --env test up` brought stack HEALTHY (scenario loader expanded ${RETRIEVAL_QA_TIMEOUT_MS} successfully — Round 71d structural pipeline GREEN); (5) `docker exec smackerel-test-smackerel-core-1 env` confirmed RETRIEVAL_QA_TIMEOUT_MS=15000 + AGENT_PROVIDER_DEFAULT_MODEL=qwen2.5:0.5b-instruct in container env; (6) warmup curl to /api/generate returned in <1s (model resident); (7) BS-002 try #1 (pre-warmup) RED with latency_ms=15030; (8) BS-002 try #2 (post-warmup) RED with latency_ms=15023 (status=saved_as_idea, error_cause=provider_unavailable — scenario timed out, capture-fallback engaged); (9) BS-007 RED with latency_ms=15065 (same timeout path engaging capture-fallback before the provenance gate could fire; expected error_cause=missing_provenance not reached). Two consecutive RED at the 15000ms budget ceiling = the exact failure mode the SCOPE-06c Honesty Note predicted. Finding routed to plan: SCOPE-06C-CPU-INTERACTIVE-15S-INFEASIBLE — qwen2.5:0.5b-instruct on this WSL2 CPU cannot complete cited retrieval-qa-v1 JSON synthesis within the 15s cpu × interactive budget. Three remediation options per Honesty Note (plan owns the decision): (a) switch cpu-interactive cell to smollm2:360m-instruct-q4_K_M (smaller, ~3-4x faster on this CPU), (b) add models.tiers.cpu.interactive.num_predict cap + thread to agent runtime (structural change), (c) accept that cpu live-stack PASS is operator-deferred to the accel-tier host alongside DoD #6 (lowest-touch; honest fit for stated 'dev loop, not production' cpu purpose). bubbles.implement recommendation: option (c). NO budget widening. NO SCOPE-06 cascade (DoD #4b/#5b/#6 require DoD #5 PASS). EDITS owned this round: (i) specs/061-conversational-assistant/report.md — appended Round 73 section with anchors #round-73-scope-06c-cpu-live-stack-red + #scope-06c-cpu-live-stack-pass (the anchor preserves SCOPE-06c DoD #5's evidence link target; the section text records the RED honestly); (ii) specs/061-conversational-assistant/scopes.md — SCOPE-06c Status In Progress (Round 71d) -> Blocked (Round 73 — cpu live-stack RED; finding routed to plan); DoD #5 line annotated with the Round 73 RED evidence + Honesty Note citation + remediation options; (iii) specs/061-conversational-assistant/state.json — this executionHistory prepend + execution.activeAgent bubbles.implement->bubbles.plan + currentPhase implement->plan + lastUpdatedAt 16:30->04:05 (next day UTC). executionHistory.summary: Round 71b (bubbles.plan; SCOPE-06c plan-triage authoring - Hardware-tier x model-role matrix body landed in scopes.md). Claim Source: advisory (planning decisions; one terminal sanity check `ls /dev/dri /dev/nvidia* /dev/accel*` returning all 'No such file or directory' confirms CPU-only WSL2 dev runtime). Outcome: completed_owned. Trigger: Round 70 (bubbles.test, HEAD a0f4842a) BLOCKED on NEW finding SCOPE-06B-D6-GEMMA3-4B-CPU-INFERENCE-EXCEEDS-60S-BUDGET (gemma3:4b warm second-call 90296 ms vs 60000 ms budget; 0.71 tok/s structural ceiling). Round 71-retest (prior bubbles.implement entry below) independently reproduced D6 (8-token resident-RAM probe >120s). Operator dispatch 2026-05-30 codified four directives: production targets Evo-X2 (Ryzen AI 9 HX 365+/Radeon 890M ROCm/XDNA2 NPU; real inference capacity); dev is WSL2 Xeon CPU-only (inference bottleneck); interactive role 5s budget on accel + relaxed on cpu; background role generous timeout always. SCOPE-06c codifies this as a 2x2 matrix (hardware tier x model role) resolved at config-generate time via fail-loud SST switch SMACKEREL_HARDWARE_TIER={cpu,accel} (no default; gitignored .smackerel.local.env overlay per machine). The 4 cells: cpu x interactive = qwen2.5:0.5b-instruct @ 15000ms (Round 65 1.5 tok/s evidence x 15s ~= 22 tokens; smallest defensible dev-loop budget; honest math); cpu x background = gemma3:4b @ 300000ms; accel x interactive = <operator-pick recommended gemma3:4b on Radeon 890M ROCm> @ 5000ms (restores design.md S5.1 production contract); accel x background = <operator-pick recommended gemma3:12b-class> @ 300000ms. executionHistory.summary: Round 65 (bubbles.implement; SCOPE-06a D4 hybrid fix attempt — Options (a) sustained warmup + (b) OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=-1). Claim Source: executed. Outcome: blocked — D4 unresolved; surfaces new defect D5 (hardware-bound per-token CPU inference latency). Hybrid plumbing implemented and verified GREEN at every plumbing layer: (1) config/smackerel.yaml gained infrastructure.ollama.keep_alive='5m' (base) and infrastructure.ollama.test.keep_alive='-1' + infrastructure.ollama.test.prewarm_warmup_num_predict=64 + infrastructure.ollama.test.prewarm_warmup_second_call_max_ms=3000 (all SST, no fallbacks); (2) scripts/commands/config.sh wires all three new env vars via required_value and emits them into the generated env (test grep: OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=-1, OLLAMA_TEST_PREWARM_WARMUP_NUM_PREDICT=64, OLLAMA_TEST_PREWARM_SECOND_CALL_MAX_MS=3000; dev grep: OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=5m, warmup knobs empty as expected for non-test); (3) docker-compose.yml adds fail-loud OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=${OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE:?...} to the ollama service environment; docker inspect smackerel-test-ollama-1 confirms OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=-1 in container env; (4) scripts/runtime/stack.sh prewarm_warm_chat_model rewritten — takes num_predict + call_label args, measures latency_ms via date +%s%N, prints summary 'warmup summary first_call_ms=X second_call_ms=Y threshold_ms=Z', issues TWO consecutive warmup calls, fail-loud exit 3 with named error when second_call_ms > OLLAMA_TEST_PREWARM_SECOND_CALL_MAX_MS. Live prewarm execution proof (set -a; source config/generated/test.env; bash scripts/runtime/stack.sh): first_call latency_ms=67333 (cold load + 64 tok), second_call latency_ms=42614 (model resident; only ~25s saved vs first), PREWARM_EXIT=3. Reading the evidence: keep_alive worked (model stayed resident — otherwise delta would be ~0), but per-token CPU inference of qwen2.5:0.5b-instruct on this hardware is ~1.5 tok/s, so 64 tokens alone consume ~43 s regardless of warmth. Retrieval-qa-v1 timeout_ms=5000 budget allows at most ~7 generated tokens — structurally infeasible on this hardware. D4 NOT closed; D5 surfaced (SCOPE-06A-D5-CPU-INFERENCE-LATENCY-EXCEEDS-RETRIEVAL-QA-BUDGET). Three non-overlapping remediation paths laid out in report.md (reselect test-tier model / per-env timeout override / cap retrieval-qa num_predict) — all require foreign-owned planning/design artifact changes, NOT taken this round. Discipline gates: ./smackerel.sh test unit UNIT_EXIT=0 (464 py-unit + Go unit passed), format --check pre-existing ml/tests/test_processor.py finding only, artifact-lint PASSED, test stack torn down clean. ACTUAL ROOT CAUSE (Claim Source: executed): test-isolation race. 5 of the 7 new tests in internal/assistant/facade_disambig_resolver_test.go called t.Parallel() and asserted before/after deltas on the same package-global Prometheus counter DisambigOutcomeResolvedUser{transport="telegram"}. Sibling increments from neighboring parallel tests leaked into each test's sandwich window. Confirmed by: (a) failing test passing in isolation (`go test -run TestFacade_DisambigResolved_TextNumericFallback_EmitsResolvedUser` → PASS); (b) running `-count=10 -parallel=16 -run 'TestFacade_DisambigResolved'` against the unfixed file produced sporadic failures on different siblings (some runs failed on TextNumericFallback, others on SaveAsNote) — classic parallel-race fingerprint, not a deterministic duplicate-increment bug. FIX APPLIED (Claim Source: executed, internal/assistant/facade_disambig_resolver_test.go): removed t.Parallel() from 5 counter-asserting tests (TypedReply, TextNumericFallback, SaveAsNote, TTLExpired, NonMatchingReply, NonNumericText) so they run serially within the package; kept t.Parallel() on TestFacade_BandBorderline_PersistsPendingDisambig because that test asserts persistence shape only and touches no counter. Targeted design.md amendment — ratified ONE coherent shell-e2e test-infrastructure pattern for spec 061 capability skills as a new §18 'Shell E2E Test Infrastructure for External-Provider Skills (Capability Foundation)' following §17 (Telegram webhook mode). Five decisions ratified: (D1) external-provider URL injection seam = Option A per-key SST keys assistant.skills.weather.{geocode_url,forecast_url} with ${VAR:?...} fail-loud guards; constructor takes URLs as required arguments (panic on empty); generalization rule: every future internal/agent/tools/<skill>/<provider>.go must inject ALL upstream URLs via constructor — enforced by extension to existing architecture-test fixture. (D2) Stub HTTP container = nginx:alpine declared in docker-compose.yml under profile:[test], container_name smackerel-test-stub-providers, routes /healthz + /v1/search + /v1/forecast (happy) + /always-503/* (BS-006), canned JSON fixtures mounted read-only from tests/e2e/stub-providers/fixtures, zero persistent volumes, lifecycle owned by ./smackerel.sh test e2e (compose --profile test up/down). Adversarial production-safety guard: internal/config/assistant.go rejects startup if URL contains 'stub-providers' AND environment != 'test'. (D3) Assertion mechanism = Option A slog-line scrape; shell e2e scrapes 'docker logs smackerel-test-smackerel-core-1' for slog.Info("assistant_turn",...) lines matching a per-test correlation_id; NO SCOPE-08 dependency, NO test-only HTTP surface. Required additive slog fields on the existing assistant_turn line: correlation_id, scenario_id, status, error_cause, user_id, transport, body_redacted. (D4) Correlation ID propagation = use Telegram update.update_id as the nonce, carried through TransportMetadata['telegram_update_id'] map on the existing §2.1 AssistantMessage contract (zero contract surface change); facade extracts and stamps correlation_id on the slog line; production-safe fallback to generated ULID when absent. (D5) Documentation impact = docs/smackerel.md needs (a) Test Stack Composition note for stub-providers container, (b) Adding-a-New-Provider-Backed-Skill operator workflow, (c) Skill E2E Assertion Pattern section — owned by bubbles.docs not this agent.
